methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The exchange rate of LP shares to base and quote tokens must never decrease.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the share price decreases, an attacker could manipulate the pool to mint shares at a discount or force existing LPs to absorb a loss, diluting their value.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_sharePriceNonDecreasing() public view { assert(baseReserve * oldTotalSupply >= oldBaseReserve * totalSupply); }

invariants.description: The total tracked staking token balance must exactly equal the sum of locked and unlocked supplies.
invariants.attack_scenario: An accounting bug allows users to lock or withdraw more tokens than they deposited, leading to insolvency of the staking contract.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_stakingTokenConservation() public view { assert(stakingTokenBalance == unlockedSupply + lockedSupply); }

invariants.description: The contract must hold enough reward tokens to cover the remaining unissued rewards for the current epoch plus all unclaimed user rewards.
invariants.attack_scenario: The operator notifies a reward amount but fails to transfer enough tokens, or tokens are skimmed, leaving the contract insolvent and unable to pay stakers.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_rewardSolvency(address rewardToken) public view { assert(IERC20(rewardToken).balanceOf(address(this)) >= pendingRewards(rewardToken)); }

invariants.description: The global locked and unlocked supplies must equal the sum of all individual user locked and unlocked balances.
invariants.attack_scenario: A logic error in lock expiration or transfer allows global supply to desync from user balances, potentially trapping funds or allowing over-withdrawal.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_supplyMatchesUserBalances() public view { assert(lockedSupply == sumLocked && unlockedSupply == sumUnlocked); }

invariants.description: The contract's actual token balances must always be greater than or equal to the tracked reserves.
invariants.attack_scenario: If balances fall below reserves, the AMM pricing curve is broken and the pool becomes insolvent, preventing LPs from withdrawing their fair share.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_balancesCoverReserves() public view { assert(baseToken.balanceOf(address(this)) >= _BASE_RESERVE_ && quoteToken.balanceOf(address(this)) >= _QUOTE_RESERVE_); }

invariants.description: The PMM R-state must correctly reflect the relationship between reserves and targets. If R is BELOW_ONE, base reserve must be >= base target. If R is ABOVE_ONE, quote reserve must be >= quote target.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the R-state is desynced from the reserves, the pricing curve will offer incorrect prices, allowing arbitrageurs to drain value from the pool.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_validRState() public view { if(_RState_ == BELOW_ONE) assert(_BASE_RESERVE_ >= _BASE_TARGET_); if(_RState_ == ABOVE_ONE) assert(_QUOTE_RESERVE_ >= _QUOTE_TARGET_); }
